channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: BuzzFeed debuts AI slop apps in bid for new revenue
item.description: BuzzFeed unveiled new AI-powered social apps at SXSW, but its demos drew muted reactions.

item.title: Execution-Time Governance: The Missing Layer in DevOps Systems
item.description: Execution-Time Governance is not a policy layer. Execution-Time Governance is the control point at the moment a system acts. Most DevOps systems optimize for deployment speed, observability, and...

item.title: AgentSift Just Grew Up: From Search Engine to Agent Intelligence Platform
item.description: You know how every AI project starts the same way? "I just need to find the right tool." Then it's 3 AM and you're reading the 47th MCP server README trying to figure out if this one will actually...

item.title: Build and Share Your Personal Portfolio with HtmlSave – Fast & Beginner-Friendly
item.description: Building and sharing a personal portfolio website doesn't have to be complicated or expensive—especially if you're a beginner, student, freelancer, or hobbyist just starting out with HTML, CSS,...

item.title: Human-in-the-Loop Evaluation Systems for GenAI Platforms
item.description: While automated evaluation pipelines and synthetic datasets provide scale, human-in-the-loop (HITL) systems remain the ground truth for production-grade Generative AI. In a stochastic environment,...

item.title: Voice Ownership Needs an Exit Door, Not Just an Onboarding Flow
item.description: Most voice AI products are optimized for onboarding. Upload samples. Verify identity. Click consent. Train the model. Connect payouts. That is the easy part. The real test of ownership starts...

item.title: Webpack vs Vite: The Complete Guide Every Frontend Dev Must Read (2026)
item.description: Before we dive in — this article explains everything from scratch. Why bundlers exist, how they work internally, and which one you should pick today. Code examples + interview-ready answers...

item.title: SurfaceDocs + LlamaIndex: From RAG Pipeline to Shareable Report
item.description: SurfaceDocs + LlamaIndex: From RAG Pipeline to Shareable Report Your RAG pipeline answers questions beautifully. It retrieves the right chunks, synthesizes a coherent response, even cites its...

item.title: User experience signals to log for mobile observability
item.description: The green dashboard fallacy Every mobile engineer has experienced this scenario in some form or another: your backend health is green, your crash free rate is at 99.9%. Yet, App Store reviews are...

item.title: The messiah rises in Dune: Part Three’s new trailer
item.description: First comes love, then comes marriage, then comes a portentous set of twin babies in the new trailer for Dune: Part Three. Warner Bros. has debuted its first full trailer for Denis Villeneuve's...

item.title: Prompt Management Is Infrastructure: Requirements, Tools, and Patterns
item.description: Mission Log #6 — Prompt control center: from strings in code to a production-grade system. If your LLM service keeps prompts in code or in a UI without strict version control, you're accumulating...

item.title: Spice Data (YC S19) Is Hiring a Product Specialist

item.title: State of Open Source on Hugging Face: Spring 2026
Now everyone in the US is getting Google’s personalized Gemini AI
item.description: Google announced on Tuesday that all users in the US will now have access to its Personal Intelligence feature, which lets you connect various Google apps to provide context for Gemini's responses...

item.title: Google’s Personal Intelligence feature is expanding to all US users
item.description: Personal Intelligence allows Google's AI assistant to tap into your Google ecosystem, such as Gmail and Google Photos, to provide more tailored responses.

item.title: Bringing the power of Personal Intelligence to more people
item.description: Bubble that says "Personal Intelligence" with Google G, Google Photos logo, and Gmail logo around it

item.title: Our latest investment in open source security for the AI era
item.description: A collage including security icons and photos of hands clasped, a man looking at a computer, and two people pointing at something off camera

item.title: Intel announces Core Ultra 200HX Plus CPUs for high-end gaming laptops
item.description: Intel has a pair of new flagship CPUs coming to a variety of pricey gaming laptops: the Core Ultra 9 290 HX Plus and Core Ultra 7 270HX Plus. The Arrow Lake Refresh chips sport 24 cores / 24...

item.title: OpenAI expands government footprint with AWS deal, report says
item.description: OpenAI has reportedly signed a partnership with AWS to sell its AI systems to the U.S. government for classified and unclassified work, marking an expansion beyond its Pentagon deal last month.

item.title: Two more EVs for the trash heap: Volvo EX30 and Honda Prologue
item.description: The steady stream of news about automakers cancelling or discontinuing electric vehicles continues apace. This week it's Volvo's small, quirky EX30 and Honda's solo electric offering in the US,...

item.title: Several Sonos audio products got their first 2026 discounts
item.description: Amazon’s Big Spring Sale kicks off on March 25th, though some great tech sales are already available. Sonos is one of several companies that has switched on sales for its products, including some...

item.title: Microsoft appoints a new Copilot boss after AI leadership shake-up
item.description: Microsoft is doing another executive shuffle today to reorganize how it engineers its Copilot assistant. Different teams have been working on the consumer and commercial sides of Copilot for...

item.title: The future of code is exciting and terrifying
item.description: Suddenly it seems like everyone's a coder. Or, at the very least, like they play one in the Claude Code app. But even for the seasoned pros, the act of software development is changing fast - many...

item.title: AI’s ‘boys’ club’ could widen the wealth gap for women, says Rana el Kaliouby
item.description: AI investor Rana el Kaliouby warns that if women are shut out of AI funding and leadership, the consequences will be grim.

item.title: Starfield is coming to the PS5 and getting a pair of major updates in April
item.description: After lots of rumors, it's now official: Bethesda's sci-fi epic Starfield is coming to the PS5. It'll launch on Sony's console on April 7th, and that day will also see the debut of two major...

item.title: Tumblr updated reblogs to function more like tweets and users hate it
item.description: Tumblr has overhauled its reblogging feature to add new options for interacting with chained posts, and it hasn't gone down well with many of the platform's users. The update announced by Tumblr...

item.title: World launches tool to verify humans behind AI shopping agents
item.description: As AI agents take the reins for online shoppers, Sam Altman's unconventional startup is looking to expand its verification offerings to support agentic commerce.

item.title: Capcom’s next big game explores the horrors of AI
item.description: Capcom has been on a hot streak. The Monster Hunter series became a global hit with the launch of Monster Hunter World in 2018, which became Capcom's best-selling game, while Street Fighter...

item.title: LeakNet Ransomware Uses ClickFix via Hacked Sites, Deploys Deno In-Memory Loader
item.description: The ransomware operation known as LeakNet has adopted the ClickFix social engineering tactic delivered through compromised websites as an initial access method. The use of ClickFix, where users...

item.title: Niv-AI exits stealth to wring more power performance out of GPUs
item.description: The company raised $12 million in seed funding to measure and manage GPU power surges.
